audit_status.review_note: Reviewed for Jacob/Esau encounter, bowing, embrace, face language, gift/blessing acceptance, reconciliation, Succoth, Shechem purchase, and El-Elohe-Israel altar.

verses.restored: Jacob lifted his eyes and saw, and look, Esau was coming, and with him four hundred men. He divided the children among Leah, Rachel, and the two slave-women.

familiar.text: And Jacob lifted up his eyes, and looked, and, behold, Esau came...

verses.restored: He put the slave-women and their children first, Leah and her children after them, and Rachel and Joseph last.

familiar.text: And he put the handmaids and their children foremost...

notes.title: Household order
notes.body: The order reveals Jacob’s hierarchy of concern within the household.

verses.restored: He himself passed over before them and bowed to the ground seven times, until he came near to his brother.

familiar.text: And he passed over before them, and bowed himself to the ground seven times...

verses.restored: Esau ran to meet him, embraced him, fell on his neck, kissed him, and they wept.

familiar.text: And Esau ran to meet him, and embraced him...

notes.title: Unexpected embrace
notes.body: The feared attack becomes embrace and tears.

verses.restored: He lifted his eyes and saw the women and the children, and said, Who are these with you? He said, The children whom the Divine has graciously given your servant.

familiar.text: And he lifted up his eyes, and saw the women and the children...

verses.restored: The slave-women came near, they and their children, and bowed down.

familiar.text: Then the handmaidens came near, they and their children...

verses.restored: Leah also came near with her children, and they bowed down. Afterward Joseph and Rachel came near, and they bowed down.

familiar.text: And Leah also with her children came near...

verses.restored: He said, What do you mean by all this camp that I met? He said, To find favor in the eyes of my lord.

familiar.text: And he said, What meanest thou by all this drove which I met?...

verses.restored: Esau said, I have much, my brother. Let what is yours be yours.

familiar.text: And Esau said, I have enough, my brother; keep that thou hast unto thyself.

verses.restored: Jacob said, No, please, if now I have found favor in your eyes, then take my gift from my hand, for I have seen your face as one sees the face of the Divine, and you have received me favorably.

familiar.text: And Jacob said, Nay, I pray thee, if now I have found grace...

notes.title: Face of the Divine
notes.body: After Peniel, Jacob sees Esau’s reconciled face as like seeing the Divine face.

verses.restored: Please take my blessing that has been brought to you, because the Divine has dealt graciously with me and because I have everything. He urged him, and he took it.

familiar.text: Take, I pray thee, my blessing that is brought to thee...

notes.title: My blessing
notes.body: Jacob gives a blessing/gift back to Esau, symbolically answering the stolen blessing story.

verses.restored: He said, Let us journey and go, and I will go before you.

familiar.text: And he said, Let us take our journey, and let us go...

verses.restored: He said to him, My lord knows that the children are tender, and that the flocks and herds nursing are with me. If they are driven hard one day, all the flocks will die.

familiar.text: And he said unto him, My lord knoweth that the children are tender...

verses.restored: Please let my lord pass over before his servant, and I will lead on slowly, according to the pace of the livestock before me and the pace of the children, until I come to my lord in Seir.

familiar.text: Let my lord, I pray thee, pass over before his servant...

verses.restored: Esau said, Please let me leave with you some of the people who are with me. He said, Why this? Let me find favor in the eyes of my lord.

familiar.text: And Esau said, Let me now leave with thee some of the folk...

verses.restored: So Esau returned that day on his way to Seir.

familiar.text: So Esau returned that day on his way unto Seir.

verses.restored: Jacob journeyed to Succoth, built himself a house, and made booths for his livestock. Therefore the name of the place was called Succoth.

familiar.text: And Jacob journeyed to Succoth, and built him an house...

verses.restored: Jacob came in peace to the city of Shechem, which is in the land of Canaan, when he came from Paddan-Aram, and he camped before the city.

familiar.text: And Jacob came to Shalem, a city of Shechem...

notes.title: In peace / Shalem
notes.body: The Hebrew may be read as safely/in peace or as Shalem, a place-name. This rendering preserves the peace motif after Jacob’s vow.

verses.restored: He bought the portion of the field where he had stretched his tent from the hand of the sons of Hamor, father of Shechem, for one hundred qesitah.

familiar.text: And he bought a parcel of a field...

verses.restored: He set up an altar there and called it El-Elohe-Israel, God, the Divine of Israel.

familiar.text: And he erected there an altar, and called it EleloheIsrael.

notes.title: El-Elohe-Israel
notes.body: The altar name links Jacob’s new name Israel with the Divine.
